title: Traditional vs. Modern Project Management Practices
description: A side-by-side comparison diagram illustrating the key differences in approach, structure, and philosophy between traditional (e.g., Waterfall) and modern (e.g., Agile/Scrum) software project management methodologies.
relationships: Traditional follows a sequential, phase-gated linear path
Modern follows an iterative, cyclical path with continuous feedback
Traditional communication is formal and scheduled
Modern communication is continuous and collaborative
